title: Real-Time Stock Updates with WebSockets
statement:
Design a WebSocket endpoint to broadcast real-time stock updates to all connected clients. 
   Implement the endpoint `/api/stock/{symbol}` to handle WebSocket connections, subscribe clients to stock updates, and broadcast the latest stock price to all connected clients.
   
   You will need to handle the following scenarios:
   
   - When a client connects to the `/api/stock/{symbol}` endpoint, they subscribe to the stock updates.
   - When a client disconnects, they unsubscribe from the stock updates.
   - When the server receives a new stock price for a subscribed stock, it broadcasts the update to all connected clients.
   
   You can use a mock stock price data source for testing purposes. The stock price data should be stored in an object and have the following properties: `symbol`, `price`.
   
   You can use the `ws` library to create a WebSocket server. The starter code provides a basic setup for the WebSocket endpoint.
   
   Ensure that your implementation is robust and handles edge cases. You should also include test cases to verify the correctness of your implementation.
constraints: Must use the `ws` library to create a WebSocket server.
Must store mock stock price data in an object with `symbol` and `price` properties.

endpoints.description: WebSocket endpoint to handle connections, subscriptions, and broadcasts
